article.description: — Anfernee Simons had 28 points and out six assists to lead the Portland Trail Blazers to a 114-112 victory over the Utah Jazz on Monday night.
article.headline: Anfernee Simons scores 28, Trail Blazers beat Jazz 114-112 in opener of a 7-game trip

images.caption: Portland Trail Blazers vs. Utah Jazz: Game Highlights
images.name: Portland Trail Blazers vs. Utah Jazz: Game Highlights

article.linkText: Anfernee Simons scores 28, Trail Blazers beat Jazz 114-112 in opener of a 7-game trip
article.story: SALT LAKE CITY -- — Anfernee Simons had 28 points and out six assists to lead the Portland Trail Blazers to a 114-112 victory over the Utah Jazz on Monday night. Jerami Grant scored 20 points, Avdija added 18 points and 14 rebounds, and Scoot Henderson finished with 17 points and six assists as Portland snapped a three-game road losing streak on the first game of a seven-game trip. Brice Sensabaugh led Utah with 23 points. Keyonte George added 21 points and Kyle Filipowski had 20 points and 11 rebounds as Utah lost for the sixth time in eight games. Henderson had a pair of baskets and assisted on two others on four straight possessions to help Portland to a 99-92 lead with 7:19 left. The Jazz closed within 113-112 with 3.6 seconds left after George hit back-to-back 3-pointers. Grant converted 1 of 2 free throws to extend the lead to two points before George missed a contested 3-pointer at the buzzer. Utah led 48-43 in the second quarter before Portland went closed out the half on an 18-3 run for a 61-51 lead at the break. Simons fueled the run with five baskets and an assist. Isaiah Collier hit a running halfcourt heave that beat the buzzer at the end of the second quarter and ended a six-minute scoring drought for the Jazz. Takeaways Trail Blazers: Portland capitalized on Utah’s mistakes, scoring 23 points on 15 turnovers. Jazz: Utah made 18 3-pointers and shot 41% from the perimeter, yet struggled to finish at the rim. Key moment George hit back-to-back 3s in a span of eight seconds, but failed to knock down a third from 3-point range at the buzzer. Key stat Avdija and Donovan Clingan combined for 26 rebounds. Up next Both teams are in action on Wednesday. Portland is at Washington, while Utah hosts Sacramento. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/nba

articles.description: The San Antonio Spurs enter Game 1 of the NBA Finals against the New York Knicks as the favorites to win the NBA championship.
articles.headline: NBA championship and Finals MVP odds: Spurs favored vs. Knicks entering Finals

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama (center left) and the San Antonio Spurs are favored entering the NBA Finals.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Victor Wembanyama are Nos. 1 and 2 in the Finals MVP odds, respectively.

images.caption: The Knicks are looking to end the series against the Cavaliers tonight, as they have the second-shortest title odds and are an overwhelming favorite to win the East.

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]
